content: We can frame Self-Refine as the incremental 'tactical work' of iterative self-improvement for LLM outputs, while LLM self-reflection mechanisms act as the strategic 'shift in perspective' that unlocks meaningful improvement, avoiding the stagnation that comes from single-perspective iterative refinement. By injecting multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives into the Self-Refine feedback loop, we can avoid the common failure mode of LLMs repeating and polishing their own initial mistakes instead of fixing them.
